title: Editas Medicine, BMS Extend R&D Collaboration for Cancer, Autoimmune Cell Therapies
text: Editas Medicine and Bristol Myers Squibb extended their research and development collaboration for cancer and autoimmune cell therapies. Under the collaboration, Editas continues development of alpha-beta T-cell medicines with Bristol-Myers-Squibb and gamma-delta T-cell medicines with Immatics N.V. The partnership extension through 2026 demonstrates continued commitment by BMS to CRISPR-based cell therapy approaches. Bristol Myers Squibb has been a major collaborator since 2014, providing significant funding and milestone opportunities for Editas' gene-edited cell therapy programs. This collaboration represents important non-dilutive funding source for Editas as company focuses resources on priority programs following strategic repositioning toward in vivo gene editing.
